evals.name: encrypt-string-stdout
evals.prompt: Encrypt the string 'hello world' using KMS key 7906979c-8e06-46a2-be2d-68e3ccbc****, output to stdout
evals.expected_output: Three lines of base64-encoded data: line1 is CiphertextBlob, line2 is 12-byte nonce, line3 is ciphertext

evals.name: encrypt-file-to-file
evals.prompt: Encrypt the file /tmp/secret.txt to /tmp/secret.enc using KMS key 7906979c-8e06-46a2-be2d-68e3ccbc****
evals.expected_output: File /tmp/secret.enc created with 3-line base64 envelope format

evals.name: encrypt-with-context
evals.prompt: Encrypt the string 'sensitive data' using KMS key 7906979c-8e06-46a2-be2d-68e3ccbc**** with encryption context {"app":"billing","stage":"prod"}
evals.expected_output: Three lines of base64-encoded data, EncryptionContext passed to KMS GenerateDataKey API

evals.name: encrypt-stdin-pipe
evals.prompt: Read from stdin and encrypt using KMS key 7906979c-8e06-46a2-be2d-68e3ccbc****, output to stdout
evals.expected_output: Three lines of base64-encoded envelope data read from stdin

evals.name: encrypt-roundtrip
evals.prompt: Encrypt the string 'roundtrip test message' using KMS key 7906979c-8e06-46a2-be2d-68e3ccbc****, then verify it can be decrypted back to the original string
evals.expected_output: Encrypted output is valid 3-line format, decrypt returns original plaintext
